title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google that replaces DOM-based agent navigation with structured tools. Agents can call named, typed, described actions directly rather than guessing site behavior through screenshots and pixel math. The standard supports both declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering custom tools.
writeup: This talk introduces WebMCP, a proposed web standard from Google that lets agents act on sites through named, typed, described tools instead of inferring behavior from the DOM, screenshots, and pixel-level math. It matters because structured tool calls replace brittle, guesswork-driven browser automation with explicit actions a site exposes directly. The standard supports two registration paths: declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs for custom tools.
